element_introduction_order.mastery_context: players familiar with Jeopardy! TV show; core mechanic is answering trivia in reverse (given answer, provide question)
player chooses from available trivia categories and difficulty tiers
higher dollar amounts indicate harder questions; players can attempt in any order within category
player must type answer in 'What is...' format; spelling and phrasing strictness varies

typical_tension_triggers: answer rejection for minor spelling/phrasing differences
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: self-paced puzzle game with no time limit; tension derives from knowledge gaps rather than time pressure; player controls rhythm entirely

difficulty_curve.early_game: player selects starting difficulty; can begin with easy or hard categories
difficulty_curve.mid_game: accumulating incorrect answers or selecting high-difficulty tiers increases challenge
difficulty_curve.late_game: remaining unsolved high-value questions present final difficulty barrier

notable_level_patterns.description: Board layout presents categories as columns and dollar values ($100-$500 or similar) as rows; player chooses intersection; no forced progression path
Unlike traditional puzzle games, no mandatory sequence; player can clear board in any order by selecting any available question
